commit.message:
feat: Tenant-Level RBAC Enforcement (#69) (#73)

* feat(rbac): permission-aware UI, permissions API, route guards, and invitations

Implements the RBAC enforcement MVP (Phases 1-3 of Issue #69):

Phase 1 - Setup:
- Create invitations table migration with RLS policies
- Update signup trigger to handle invitation flow
- Add TypeScript permission types (Permission, TenantRole, PermissionSet)
- Add Pydantic team models (CreateInvitationRequest, TeamMemberResponse, etc.)

Phase 2 - Foundation:
- GET /api/me/permissions endpoint (resolves user's role → permissions)
- Zustand permissionsStore (session-scoped, O(1) lookup via Set)
- usePermissions hook (can, canAny, canAll, role, isPlatformAdmin)
- RequirePermission component (hides UI elements)
- PermissionGuard component (blocks routes, redirects to role-aware landing)
- Install Sonner toast library + Toaster in App.tsx

Phase 3 - Permission-Aware UI:
- Sidebar navigation filtered by permission (NavItem.permission field)
- All routes wrapped with PermissionGuard in router.tsx
- Permissions fetched on login, cleared on logout

813 Python tests pass, 238 Vitest tests pass, tsc clean, ruff+mypy clean.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(rbac): team management API, service, and UI

Implements Phase 5 (US4: Team Management) of Issue #69:

Backend:
- TeamService with full team lifecycle: list, invite, cancel, resend,
  update role, remove member, transfer ownership
- Team router with 7 endpoints at /api/v1/team/*
- All operations protected by require_permission(USERS_MANAGE)
- Owner protection: cannot remove/demote owner, only owner can
  promote to admin
- Uses admin_client for cross-user queries (bypasses RLS)

Frontend:
- teamApi client (7 API methods)
- useTeam hooks (TanStack Query mutations with cache invalidation)
- TeamTab component with invite form, member list, role management,
  pending invitations
- Added 'Equipo' tab to CompanySettingsPage (wrapped with
  RequirePermission for users.manage)
- Toast notifications via Sonner for all team actions

813 Python tests pass, 238 Vitest tests pass, tsc/ruff/mypy clean.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(rbac): facturador daily activity view

Implements Phase 4 (US3: Facturador Activity View) of Issue #69:

Backend:
- GET /api/v1/dashboard/activity endpoint with DASHBOARD_ACTIVITY
  permission (available to all roles including facturador)
- Fetches today's invoices (drafts + confirmed) and delivery notes
- Facturadores see only their own activity; other roles see all
- Activity models: ActivityItem, ActivitySummary, ActivityResponse
- Uses admin_client per architecture guard (BUG-003 prevention)

Frontend:
- ActivityPage with daily summary cards (invoices, delivery notes, total)
- Live refresh every 30s for real-time activity feel
- Empty state with helpful messaging
- Sidebar: 'Mi Actividad' nav item with Activity icon
- PermissionGuard updated: facturador landing → /activity
- Route /activity protected by dashboard.activity permission

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(rbac): ownership transfer UI with dual confirmation

Implements Phase 6 (US5: Ownership Transfer) of Issue #69:

- Transfer button (ArrowRightLeft icon) shown to owner on each
  non-owner team member
- Modal dialog with warning banner explaining consequences
- Dual confirmation: user must type TRANSFERIR to proceed
- Toast notifications for success/failure
- Auto-clears confirmation state on cancel or success

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(rbac): subscription tier limits on team members

Implements Phase 7 (US6: Subscription Tier Limits) of Issue #69:

Backend:
- Migration adds subscription_tier + max_members to tenants table
- TeamService.create_invitation() checks tier limit before creating
  (counts current members + pending invitations vs max_members)
- Returns 402 Payment Required when limit exceeded
- TeamListResponse now includes tier info for frontend display

Frontend:
- Tier info bar showing X/Y members and plan name
- 'Límite alcanzado' badge when at capacity
- Invite form replaced with upgrade prompt when at limit
- Error toast for 402 responses

Defaults: basic tier with max_members=1 (owner only).
Pro tier can be set per-tenant until billing system (#27).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(rbac): comprehensive E2E tests for RBAC enforcement (29 tests)

- Add 5 E2E test suites: sidebar, route-guards, permissions-api, activity, team
- Multi-role test infrastructure: owner, admin, facturador, auditor auth states
- Global setup creates 4 users via invitation flow, global teardown cleans up

Bug fixes discovered during E2E testing:
- Fix from __future__ import annotations in models/team.py breaking Pydantic
  runtime UUID/datetime resolution (caused 500 on /api/v1/team)
- Fix signup trigger field name: 'slug' → 'tenant_slug' in 2 migrations
- Fix RLS policy type mismatch: remove ::TEXT cast on UUID comparisons

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add RBAC roles documentation (in-app help + internal reference)

- Add '👥 Roles y permisos del equipo' section to HelpPage with permission
  table, role descriptions, and team management tips
- Create docs/reference/rbac-roles.md with full architecture docs:
  permission matrix, invitation flow, E2E coverage, and future notes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: e2e_cleanup_tenant references renamed table + remove debug logging

- Fixed service_catalog_items → service_catalog in e2e_cleanup_tenant RPC
- Removed debug console.log from global-setup.ts
- Removed debug pageerror/console listeners from rbac-sidebar.spec.ts
- All 29 RBAC E2E tests pass, CI green (813 pytest + 238 vitest)

Bug: e2e_cleanup_tenant function referenced old table name
'service_catalog_items' which was renamed to 'service_catalog',
causing FK constraint cascading failures in E2E teardown.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: e2e cleanup functions — PostgREST compatibility + missing FK children

Bugs found during full E2E run (pre-existing, not from RBAC feature):

1. session_replication_role blocked by PostgREST — both e2e_cleanup_tenant
   and new e2e_delete_billing_records used SET session_replication_role which
   PostgREST denies. Replaced with ALTER TABLE DISABLE/ENABLE TRIGGER for
   each immutability trigger (billing_records, line_items, invoice_pdfs,
   aeat_submissions).

2. Missing FK child tables — e2e_cleanup_tenant didn't delete from
   billing_record_line_items, draft_line_items, delivery_note_items, or
   delivery_note_counters, causing silent FK constraint failures.

3. deleteBillingRecord() was nuclear — called e2e_cleanup_tenant which
   deletes the ENTIRE tenant. Replaced with targeted e2e_delete_billing_records
   that only removes billing data, keeping tenant/company/customers intact.

All 240 E2E tests pass. CI green (813 pytest + 238 vitest).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename *Scale() to *Distribution() for unified naming

Unify the naming convention across SDK, CLI, and web editor:
- SDK: .lightnessScale() → .lightnessDistribution() (same for saturation/hue)
- CLI: --lightness-scale → --lightness-distribution (same for saturation/hue)
- Entity: lightnessScaleType → lightnessDistributionType (same for saturation/hue)
- CLI JSON output: config.scales → config.distributions

This aligns the ramp builder API with the color space .distribution()
method, which already used the correct naming. Both accept the same
ScaleType values (linear, fibonacci, ease-in, etc.).

Updated all tests, READMEs, SKILL.md, and export functions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix mid-rotation face color flicker in hero cubes

The fragment shader was determining face color by classifying the
rotated normal against world axes. During diagonal tumbles, normals
cross axis boundaries causing faces to momentarily pick the wrong
color uniform.

Fix: bake face index (0-5) as a vertex attribute (aFI) so each
geometric face keeps its assigned color regardless of orientation.
Normals are still used for diffuse lighting, just not for color
selection.

commit.message:
perf: strongly typed render plans + single-span renderer (#1306 #1307 #1308 #1309)

Move RenderPlan, RouteRenderPlans, RenderStats from Rendering → Core:
- BmwContext.CompiledPlans is now RouteRenderPlans? (no object? cast)
- RouteHandlerData.CompiledPlans is now RouteRenderPlans? (strongly typed)
- RenderStatsProvider in Core — no circular dependency

Single-span render path (#1309):
- RenderPlan.EstimateSize() precomputes exact output byte count
- RenderPlan.ExecuteSingleSpan() renders into one contiguous Span<byte>
- RenderWithPlansSingleSpanAsync: one GetSpan, one Advance, one Flush
- WriteHtmlEncodedToSpan uses UTF-8 byte literals (u8) for entities
- Maximum cache locality, minimum PipeWriter syscall overhead

Performance annotations (#1308):
- AggressiveInlining on Execute, ExecuteSingleSpan, Bind, EstimateSize
- AggressiveInlining on WriteHtmlEncoded, WriteSpan, WriteHtmlEncodedToSpan
- Span.CopyTo already uses SIMD via Buffer.Memmove at runtime

All 178 tests pass (176 rendering + 2 core).

Disable AIA certificate downloads for server client-cert validation by default (#125049)

During TLS handshake, peers should include all intermediate certificates
necessary for the other side to perform certificate validation. If
intermediates are not sent, it is possible for the receiver to look them
up and download using the Authority Information Access extension (AIA).

This behavior is not desirable on server side for multiple reasons:
- performance - AIA server may be slow or plain unresponsive, performing
network IO as part of certificate validation slows down the handshake.
- security - server makes outbound HTTP GET requests to client-provided
endpoint.

This PR changes the default behavior of server SslStream instances to
disable intermediate cert downloading using AIA by default. Scenarios,
where user passes custom `X509ChainPolicy` are not affected.

This change may break scenarios where client and/or server is not
properly configured, meaning any of:
- client does not send necessary intermediates in the handshake (on
Windows, this requires that the CA cert is present in the CA store)
- server does not have the necessary intermediate certificates in the CA
store.

Action to be taken by affected users are either:
- configure server auth options with `X509ChainPolicy` with `ExtraStore`
that contains the necessary intermediates.
- specify `X509ChainPolicy` that either allows AIA downloads (not
recommended)
- install CA certificates to the proper certificate store, so they can
be looked up when building the outbound cert chain (client) or when
validating client cert (server)
